modelDefinitions.name: ApplicationSpanEventLog
modelDefinitions.description: The Application Span Event Log Event model will collect information about what ran a mutation. The specific Application Span Event Log Id can be found on any Application models through the _trace field. This will only capture trace information for span events that run a mutation.

fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.description: The ID of the parent span, this is not a relation. When you group by the spanId you can see all the child requests that were created under the parent.

fields.description: The service the request originates from.

fields.name: forwardedForServiceName
fields.description: The service the request was forwarded through. Through a trace, this could create a chain of services the request was forwarded through.

fields.name: outputSchema
fields.description: A JSON schema defining the shape of the output the tool will return to the LLM. The data flow output is validated against this schema prior to returning the output to the agent. This schema is used by the LLM agent to determine if this is the correct tool to call.

fields.description: If true, the tool does not modify its environment.

fields.name: destructive
fields.description: If true, the tool may perform destructive updates to its environment. If false, the tool performs only additive updates. This property is meaningful only when readOnly is set to false.

fields.name: idempotent
fields.description: If true, calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments will have no additional effect on the its environment. This property is meaningful only when readOnly is set to false.

modelDefinitions.name: SchedulingConfiguration
modelDefinitions.description: Scheduling Configuration is defined for the SchedulingConfiguration Screen Element as well as the Schedule Engine. The definition defined on a record of this model will provide loading data into the scheduler as well as committing that data back to the database. This configuration will be copied upon creation of a SchedulingConfigurationRun.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of your Scheduling Configuration. This will generally align with the use case of the Scheduling Configuration.

fields.name: schedulingWindow
fields.description: The Scheduling Window will define what part of the schedule can be changed from within the context of a scheduling run.

fields.name: dependencyMapping
fields.description: Dependencies define relations between events (Ex: A -> B when A is completed, B can be started). Dependency Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load dependencies into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: eventMapping
fields.description: Events are the range of time defined on the schedule (Ex: Work Order Process). Event Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load events into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: resourceMapping
fields.description: Resources are the units that you allocate the events to (Ex: Workcenter). Resource Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load resources into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

fields.name: assignmentMapping
fields.description: Assignments are the relation between Events and Resources, defining what is currently assigned to a resource. Assignment Mapping provides the data model and field mappings to load assignments into the Scheduler. More than one mapping can be provided referencing different models.

modelDefinitions.name: SchedulingConfigurationRun
modelDefinitions.description: The Scheduling Configuration Run represents a scheduling attempt on the resources defined by the mappings provided in the Scheduling Configuration. When a Scheduling Configuration Run is created, it will automatically begin the process of scheduling the provided resources by automatically loading them into the Scheduler, processing them and updating the run record with the changes the scheduler made. From there you can commit those changes through the `commitSchedulingConfigurationRun` mutation.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The primary unique ID of the record.  Every model should have an id field.

fields.name: label
fields.description: The label can be used to assist in searching for a specific run. If no value is provided it will be defaulted.

fields.name: startDate
fields.description: The calculated start date of the Scheduling Run based on the Configurations Scheduling window. Will always be populated by the start of the day when the run is created.

fields.name: endDate
fields.description: The calculated end date of the Scheduling Run based on the Configurations Scheduling window. Will always be populated with the startDate + schedulingWindow duration

fields.name: schedulingWindow
fields.description: The Scheduling Window will define what part of the schedule can be changed from within the context of a scheduling run. This is copied from the SchedulingConfiguration upon creation of a run.

fields.name: changes
fields.description: The changes reported by Bryntum

fields.name: filter
fields.description: An array of objects containing a dataModel and predicate property used for targeting events to be scheduled.

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationId
fields.description: The ID of the Scheduling Configuration of which this run will be created against.

fields.name: schedulingConfiguration
fields.description: The relation to the Scheduling Configuration

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationRunStatusId
fields.description: The ID of the Scheduling Configuration Run Status the Run is currently in.

fields.name: schedulingConfigurationRunStatus
fields.description: The relation to the Scheduling Configuration Run Status.

fields.name: error
fields.description: If an error occurs during scheduling or committing that error will be reported here.

fields.name: autoCommit
fields.description: When autoCommit is true, the run record will automatically update the Events model with changes provided by the scheduler. This is useful when there is no desire to review the schedule before it's committed.

fields.name: autoSchedule
fields.description: When autoSchedule is true, the Scheduling Engine will automatically solve for the provided constraints and provide a new schedule.
